summary: Iteration 2: Tested new airdrop endpoints, Pydantic validation, health, CORS, and airdrop frontend page. All 24 backend tests passed. Airdrop UI fully functional: token picker tabs (launchpad/manual), recipients tabs (paste/csv), on-chain mint info panel populates for USDC (decimals=6, supply=8.59B), validation surfaces per-line errors (Invalid wallet address, Invalid amount, Duplicate address), dry-run summary (Total tokens / Recipients / Batches / Fee), preview button correctly disabled until wallet connects.

issues: One console error 'ERR_UNKNOWN_URL_SCHEME' — likely from rendering ipfs:// image URIs directly in . Should be rewritten to gateway URL (gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/) for browser rendering.

action_items: Optional: Convert ipfs:// image URIs to https gateway in Explorer/Launchpad tags to avoid ERR_UNKNOWN_URL_SCHEME console errors.

Optional: SuccessModal share/import/copy testids (share-on-x-btn, copy-share-text-btn, import-token-btn, copy-mint-btn) were not asserted because a real token creation requires a wallet signature (cannot be triggered in headless). Main agent may self-verify via manual flow.
critical_code_review_comments: AirdropBatchRequest enforces max_length=15 recipients via Pydantic — confirmed returns 422 in test.
TokenMetadata validators (name 1-64, symbol 1-12, decimals 0-9, supply 1..10^15) all enforced — confirmed via 422 responses.
/api/airdrop/build-batch correctly rejects duplicate recipients with 400 and message containing 'duplicate'.
Built transaction size for 3 recipients (6 instructions) is well under 1232-byte cap.

CORS header `access-control-allow-origin` reflected correctly. Note: when `CORS_ORIGINS='*'` and `allow_credentials=True`, Starlette returns the request Origin (not '*'); test verified header presence which is sufficient. Recommend tightening CORS_ORIGINS in prod env (warning logged at startup).
server.py is ~1213 lines — consider splitting into routers/airdrop.py, routers/tokens.py, services/solana.py for maintainability.
derive_ata() has a confusing for-loop that always returns on first iteration via Pubkey.find_program_address — the loop body is dead code. Cleanup recommended (not a bug, just maintenance).

success_rate.frontend: 100% of reachable UI assertions (wallet-gated actions skipped as expected)

seed_data_creation: None — read-only on-chain RPC + Pydantic validation tests. Test_api.py from iteration 1 creates TEST_ prefixed tokens but was not re-run this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has 2 test files now: /app/backend/tests/test_api.py (iteration 1 - core token CRUD/IPFS) and /app/backend/tests/test_airdrop.py (iteration 2 - airdrop + Pydantic + health + CORS). Both load REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env via helper. Airdrop frontend data-testids: airdrop-page, airdrop-token-section, tab-launchpad, tab-manual-mint, launchpad-token-select, manual-mint-input, airdrop-recipients-section, tab-paste, tab-csv, recipients-textarea, airdrop-preview-btn. Mainnet USDC EPjFWdd5AufqSSqeM2qN1xzybapC8G4wEGGkZwyTDt1v works as a reliable mint-info fixture. SuccessModal share/import buttons were not verified end-to-end (requires real wallet signed tx).
rca of the issue: No issues identified in this iteration. All 24 new backend tests passed on first run after fixing BASE_URL loading (the original /app/backend/tests pattern relied on REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL being set as env var; I added a fallback that reads /app/frontend/.env). The minor explorer console warning (ERR_UNKNOWN_URL_SCHEME) is a benign side-effect of ipfs:// image URIs not being natively rendered by the browser — does not break the page.
